summary.narrative: Johnson entered office on unity platform, achieved high approval (77%) and re-election (98.7% in 2023). Party switch to Republican in Sept 2023 cited law-and-order priorities diverging from Democratic platform. Limited specific policy details available; sources emphasize public safety stance and housing affordability advocacy.
summary.grade_rationale: High voter approval and re-election suggest delivery on implicit promises, but vague campaign language and party switch without clear policy accomplishments limit assessment. Housing advocacy recent but undocumented.

promises.normalized: Move beyond divisions at City Hall and unite on shared solutions
Create strong, safe neighborhoods where families thrive and children succeed
Prioritize police support and public safety enforcement
Address housing affordability and remove regulatory roadblocks

data_gaps: Specific public safety metrics or police department initiatives documented
Housing policy details, regulatory changes proposed or implemented
Budget allocations or departmental restructuring since 2019
Neighborhood improvement programs or crime reduction data
Details on City Hall governance reforms or departmental collaboration improvements
Post-party-switch policy changes or executive orders issued
Council relationship dynamics and legislative passage rates
